LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CRUSADER INSURANCE COMPANY, DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THERE IS NO BREACH OF CONTRACT WHEN AN INSURER EXERCISES ITS RIGHT TO SETTLE A CLAIM. THERE IS NO BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING WHEN AN INSURER EXERCISES ITS EXPRESS RIGHT TO SETTLE A CLAIM. ALL OF PLAINTIFF'S CAUSES OF ACTION ARE IMPERMISSIBLY FOUNDED UPON THE INSURER'S RIGHT TO SETTLEMENT A CLAIM. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECTS MAY BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-22-2009 Continued To Jul-31-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-08-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CRUSADER INSURANCE COMPANY, DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. There is no breach of contract when an insurer exercises its right to settle a claim. There is no breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing when an insurer exercises its express right to settle a claim. All of plaintiff's causes of action are impermissibly founded upon the insurer's right to settlement a claim. No reasonable probability that defects may be cured by amendment. (Moving party to prepare Order.)(302/CWW/pm)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Claim For Punitive Damages In Pltf'S First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-08-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CRUSADER INSURANCE COMPANY, Motion To Strike Claim For Punitive Damages In Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike is off calendar as moot, in view of ruling on demurrer. (302/CWW/pm)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CRUSADER INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST, SECOND, AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION; OF COMPLAINT. Appearances: Howard M. Olsen, Esq., Attorney at Law, (415) 931-4207, appearing for Plaintiff, and opposing party; Ali Pasha Hamidi, Esq., of Cox Castle Nicholson, (415) 262-5106, appearing for Defendant, Crusader Insurance Company and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Sustained with leave to amend. No substantive opposition filed. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. 302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797
